  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which primary focus should we prioritize to \"make this stronger\"? Choose one: (A) prose-level tightening and emphasis (R1.6); (B) conceptual framing and argument strengthening—thesis/gap, logic, and claim–evidence alignment (R1.1, R1.2, R1.3, R1.5); or (C) novelty/positioning—moderating the \"first formal analysis\" claim and situating vs prior work (R1.4, R1.7).",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening likely involves sharpening the core thesis and contribution framing to foreground significance and scope.",
    "R1.2": "A stronger piece requires a coherent argument chain from assumptions to theorem to corollary without hidden leaps.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about calibration error and empirical tracking must be checked against the stated results to ensure alignment.",
    "R1.5": "Reordering or restructuring sections may better foreground the main theorem, corollary, and practical implications.",
    "R1.6": "Improving clarity, emphasis, and concision at the sentence level is a common interpretation of making a passage stronger.",
    "RX.1": "Any revisions should explicitly acknowledge assumptions and uncertainties (e.g., bounded disagreement, independence).",
    "RX.2": "We must avoid overclaiming, especially around \"first formal analysis\" and generalization beyond stated conditions.",
    "RX.3": "Work should stay within strengthening the provided text rather than extending scope to new experiments or claims.",
    "RX.4": "Care is needed to prevent readers from over-relying on the method in safety-critical settings without warranted evidence.",
    "RX.5": "Changes should be traceable to specific sentences and claims in the artifact to maintain clear provenance."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which primary focus should we prioritize to "make this stronger"? Choose one: (A) prose-level tightening and emphasis (R1.6); (B) conceptual framing and argument strengthening—thesis/gap, logic, and claim–evidence alignment (R1.1, R1.2, R1.3, R1.5); or (C) novelty/positioning—moderating the "first formal analysis" claim and situating vs prior work (R1.4, R1.7).

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening likely involves sharpening the core thesis and contribution framing to foreground significance and scope.
rationale.R1.2: A stronger piece requires a coherent argument chain from assumptions to theorem to corollary without hidden leaps.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about calibration error and empirical tracking must be checked against the stated results to ensure alignment.
rationale.R1.5: Reordering or restructuring sections may better foreground the main theorem, corollary, and practical implications.
rationale.R1.6: Improving clarity, emphasis, and concision at the sentence level is a common interpretation of making a passage stronger.
rationale.RX.1: Any revisions should explicitly acknowledge assumptions and uncertainties (e.g., bounded disagreement, independence).
rationale.RX.2: We must avoid overclaiming, especially around "first formal analysis" and generalization beyond stated conditions.
rationale.RX.3: Work should stay within strengthening the provided text rather than extending scope to new experiments or claims.
rationale.RX.4: Care is needed to prevent readers from over-relying on the method in safety-critical settings without warranted evidence.
rationale.RX.5: Changes should be traceable to specific sentences and claims in the artifact to maintain clear provenance.
